rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical radical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-allylated 2-arylbenzimidazoles with CF3SO2Na to afford CF3-substituted benzimidazo-fused products
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (C cloth anode, 1.5 mA) coupled with eosin Y photocatalysis under 455 nm to generate CF3 radical from CF3SO2Na; photoexcited eosin Y oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3• (with SO2 extrusion), and the anode regenerates the ground-state photocatalyst

key_intermediates.name: CF3 radical from CF3SO2Na (Langlois reagent) after SO2 loss
C-centered alkyl radical from CF3 addition to the allyl alkene of 1a
Cyclized cationic intermediate (radical-polar crossover; aryl-stabilized carbocation/Wheland-type)

electron_flow: Photoexcited eosin Y oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3• (releasing SO2); CF3• adds to the terminal alkene of 1a giving a C-radical, which cyclizes onto the pendant arene. The resulting cyclohexadienyl radical is oxidized (anode and/or eosin Y•+) to a cation, and deprotonation rearomatizes to product 3a; the anode regenerates eosin Y.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Trace 3a; CF3-DPE adduct 18 isolated in 36%, confirming CF3 radical

notes: Reaction profiles compare std conditions vs 'no PC, no light' and 'no electricity', supporting that both photocatalysis and electrolysis are required (synergistic photoelectrocatalysis). DPE trapping experiment isolates CF3-DPE adduct 18 (36%), providing direct evidence for a CF3 radical intermediate. Radical-polar crossover (oxidation of cyclohexadienyl radical to cation followed by rearomatization) is invoked for the cyclization step.
